content:
I am a PhD Research Scholar at the Department of Economics of NHH Norwegian School of Economics. I am affiliated to the FAIR - The Choice Lab research group. My research is in experimental and behavioral economics, with a focus on group identity, information processing and belief formation, and the effects of social context on behavior.
I will be on the Job Market in the 2023-2024 academic year.
summary: I am a PhD Research Scholar at the Department of Economics of NHH Norwegian School of Economics. I am affiliated to the FAIR - The Choice Lab research group. My research is in experimental and behavioral economics, with a focus on group identity, information processing and belief formation, and the effects of social context on behavior.
title: Daniel Carvajal

summary: This paper finds that social context, defined as the group composition of a society, is a key determinant of prosocial behavior and group identity effects.
title: Social context, identity and prosocial behavior (Job Market Paper)
Do individuals care differently about members of a majority vs a minority?
